sections.title: Overview and diagnosis
sections.content: Psoriasis diagnosis is clinical, based on typical plaques, distribution, and history. Consider skin biopsy if atypical. Check for psoriatic arthritis if joint symptoms occur.

sections.title: Topical therapies
sections.content: First-line for mild disease: emollients, corticosteroids (potent/very potent for plaques), vitamin D analogues (calcipotriol). Avoid long-term high-potency steroid use on thin skin without supervision.

sections.title: Phototherapy and systemic options
sections.content: Narrowband UVB phototherapy is effective for widespread disease. Systemic agents include methotrexate, cyclosporine, acitretin, and biologic agents targeting TNF, IL-17, IL-23 pathways for moderate-to-severe psoriasis.
